channel.title: The Brussels Times
channel.description: Latest Brussels posts from The Brussels Times.

item.title: City of Brussels to make live-out landlords pay more property tax
item.description: In the City of Brussels, only 30% of properties are owner-occupied.

item.title: Multilingualism in Belgium: What happens in our brains when we switch languages?
item.description: For residents of Brussels, switching between languages is a part of everyday life – but it comes with a heavy cognitive workload.

item.title: Police urged to act amid alarming increase in e-scooter deaths and injuries in Belgium
item.description: Brussels is banning shared e-scooters from 2027 and new data on road traffic accidents helps to explain why.

item.title: Wallonia led Belgium's new business growth last year - here's why
item.description: Wallonia recorded a 12.1% growth in new firms in 2025, with Hainaut recording an increase of almost 20%.

item.title: Letters from Europe's darkest chapter could soon be etched on a new Brussels memorial
item.description: If plans come together as expected, Brussels will host a major new place of remembrance.

item.title: Brussels' green spaces reopen following weekend thunderstorms
item.description: The regional parks, woods and nature reserves had been closed as a precaution on Saturday evening.

item.title: Flemish universities raise tuition fees for non-EU students
item.description: The universities say the increases are a response to spending cuts introduced by Flemish education minister.

item.title: Police search for suspects behind Brussels shootings
item.description: The police are looking for suspects following two shootings on Sunday night in the Brussels.

item.title: 'Let's ignore media noise': US marks 250th anniversary with celebrations in Brussels
item.description: Donald Trump made a brief appearance via video link at the Cinquantenaire event, and received a special gift from the Antwerp diamond industry.

item.title: Disruptions to Brussels tram 44 following weekend storm
item.description: The tram 44 services are disrupted due to damage caused by the storm over the weekend.

item.title: 'Love isn't a finite resource': Meet the polyamorous Belgians of Brussels
item.description: "The running gag in the polyamorous community is that the most important thing is knowing how to use Google Calendar."

item.title: 'More people own pets': Brussels' 24/7 veterinary emergency clinic sparked by growing demand
item.description: The new centre in Ixelles offers round-the-clock emergency and specialist care for all types of pets, including exotic ones.

item.title: 'Small outbreak’ of mpox in Brussels, but how serious is the risk?
item.description: Brussels saw a “small outbreak” of the viral disease mpox in April and May. Here is what you need to know.

item.title: Why Brussels is so much hotter than its surrounding countryside
item.description: A temperature difference of almost 23°C between Brussels city centre and the nearby Sonian Forest has laid bare the dramatic impact of the urban heat island effect.

item.title: We're all irritable in this heat – but there's a crucial difference between here and my home country
item.description: How can you be ‘positive’ when you feel like a giant chocolate Manneken Pis melting into a puddle? Health coach Kristina Chetchuti is here to put your mind at rest.

item.title: Traffic disruptions in Brussels this weekend for US party in Cinquantenaire Park
item.description: Drivers should expect significant traffic disruptions around Cinquantenaire Park on Sunday, 28 June as the US celebrates its 250th anniversary in the park.

item.title: What to do in Brussels this weekend: 26 - 28 June
item.description: A heatwave has not stopped a weekend full of festivals, performances and expositions. Here are our favourite picks!

item.title: Feel the heat? Everything you need to know about Couleur Café 2026
item.description: Brussels' Couleur Café music festival will bring the heat in more ways than one when it returns under high temperatures this weekend.

item.title: Extra bins and rat traps: How Brussels plans to clean up the city centre this summer
item.description: As terraces fill up, tourists flock to the city and major events return for the summer season, public spaces in Brussels come under increasing pressure.

item.title: Residents unable to open windows due to smell from deadly Anderlecht warehouse fire
item.description: The foul odours have become increasingly noticeable as temperatures rise, raising serious concerns amid Belgium's current heatwave.
